title: SigLinDe Parameters and Weekday Factors for Gas SLP
text: BDEW Leitfaden defines 15 gas standard load profiles. Three residential (prefix ‘H’ “Haushalt”) twelve commercial industrial (prefix ‘G’ “Gewerbe”). Table 1: BDEW gas standard load profile identifiers descriptions.

title: SigLinDe Parameters and Weekday Factors for Gas SLP
text: Daily gas consumption computed using SigLinDe method (Sigmoid + Linear + Deutschland). dimensionless daily heating demand \(h(\vartheta)\) daily temperature \(\vartheta\) (°C) : \[h(\vartheta) = \frac{}{1 + \left(\frac{B}{\vartheta - \vartheta_0}\right)^C} + D + \max\left(m_H \cdot \vartheta + b_H,\; m_W \cdot \vartheta + b_W\right)\] profile set nine coefficients. linear component \(\max(m_H \vartheta + b_H,\; m_W \vartheta + b_W)\) represents envelope space-heating line (Heizgas-Gerade) hot-water line (Warmwasser-Gerade). parameters differ variant 34 (57 % linear component, default) variant 33 (45 % linear component). Use slp_gas_coefficients() retrieve values R:
code: slp_gas_coefficients()                        # all 15 profiles, both variants (30 rows) slp_gas_coefficients(variant = "34")         # all profiles, variant 34 only slp_gas_coefficients("HEF")                  # one profile, both variants slp_gas_coefficients("HEF", variant = "33")  # one profile, one variant

text: Gas consumption strongly correlated outdoor temperature; colder gets, gas consumed — least gas used space heating. relationship outside temperature gas consumption captured profile function \(h(\vartheta)\): \[ h(\vartheta) = \underbrace{\frac{}{1 + \left(\dfrac{B}{\vartheta - \vartheta_0}\right)^C} + D}_{\text{sigmoid part}} + \underbrace{\max\!\left(m_H \vartheta + b_H,\; m_W \vartheta + b_W\right)}_{\text{linear part}} \] function initially published 2003 S-shaped sigmoid function:6 \[ h(\vartheta) = \frac{}{1 + \left(\dfrac{B}{\vartheta - \vartheta_0}\right)^C} + D \] Since , revised part ongoing debate. research paper 2015 found pure sigmoid approach systematically underestimated consumption cold temperatures. authors proposed improved formulation called SigLinDe (Sigmoid + Linear + Deutschland) published SigLinDe coeffeciants 15 different customer groups, .e. profile IDs.7 SigLinDe adds linear component sigmoid equation representing space-heating demand (German: Heizgas-Gerade) hot water demand (German: Warmwasser-Gerade). title: Gas Standard Load Profiles (Standardlastprofile Gas)
text: kundenwert accounts customer’s individual consumption behaviour. value answers question: much gas consumed customer day outside temperature 8°C? scaling factor kWh/day — 8°C, point \(h(\vartheta) = 1\) every profile. kundenwert derived customer’s annual consumption year’s temperature weekday profile. Starting \(\eqref{eq:qdaily}\), annual total consumption sum daily values: \[ Q_a = \sum_{D} Q(D) = \sum_{D} h(\vartheta_D) \times KW \times F_{WT,D} \] Rearranging solve \(KW\): \[ KW = \frac{Q_a}{\displaystyle\sum_{D} h(\vartheta_D) \times F_{WT,D}} \tag{2}\label{eq:kw} \] \(Q_a\) annual gas consumption (German: Jahresverbrauch) \(\vartheta_D\) daily temperature day \(D\) \(F_{WT,D}\) weekday factor day \(D\) Using example single-family home (HEF) Düsseldorf annual consumption 15,000 kWh — customer README — table shows denominator built , day day, Düsseldorf long-term daily mean temperatures (2004–2024): sum denominator across 365 days gives \(\sum_D h(\vartheta_D) = 272.32\). since three residential profiles weekday factors 1, \(\eqref{eq:kw}\) simplifies : \[ KW = \frac{Q_a}{\displaystyle\sum_{D} h(\vartheta_D)} \] \(KW \approx\) 15,000 / 272.32 = 55.1 kWh/day.

title: Gas Standard Load Profiles (Standardlastprofile Gas)
text: section gives quick introduction rdwd package, read features brry.github.io/rdwd. workflow three steps: selectDWD() — find download URL station dataset. dataDWD() — download unzip file. readDWD() — parse file data frame. need daily climate summary (var = "kl", res = "daily"). column TMK contains daily mean temperature °C. find exact station name pass selectDWD(), use findID(): reference temperatures use long-term daily mean 2004–2024 rather single year, individual-year anomaly distorts scaling factor11. Now reference temperature series, can call slp_gas_kundenwert(): value README example passes slp_gas() generate 2025/26 heating season — use ask customer fare different climate.
code: # returns matching station names and IDs rdwd::findID("Duesseldorf", exactmatch = FALSE) library(rdwd)  # Full daily climate record for Düsseldorf (DWD station 1078) links <- selectDWD(   "Duesseldorf",   res = "daily",   var = "kl",   per = c("historical", "recent"),   current = TRUE )  raw <- do.call(   rbind,   readDWD(dataDWD(links, read = FALSE, force = TRUE), varnames = FALSE) )  # drop historical/recent overlap raw <- raw[!duplicated(raw$MESS_DATUM), ]     # Long-term mean daily temperature over 2004-2024, one value per calendar day ref       <- raw[format(raw$MESS_DATUM, "%Y") %in% as.character(2004:2024), ] ref$mmdd  <- format(ref$MESS_DATUM, "%m-%d") clim      <- aggregate(TMK ~ mmdd, data = ref, FUN = mean) clim      <- clim[clim$mmdd != "02-29", ]          # drop the leap day dates_ref <- as.Date(paste0("2023-", clim$mmdd))   # any non-leap year temps_ref <- clim$TMK # kundenwert for a 15,000 kWh/a HEF customer slp_gas_kundenwert("HEF", dates_ref, temps_ref, annual_consumption = 15000) #>      HEF  #> 55.08305

title: Generate a Standard Load Profile for Electricity — slp_electricity
code: start <- "2026-01-01" end <- "2026-12-31"  # multiple profile IDs are supported L <- slp_electricity(c("L0", "L1", "L2"), start, end) head(L) #>   profile_id          start_time            end_time watts #> 1         L0 2026-01-01 00:00:00 2026-01-01 00:15:00  68.3 #> 2         L0 2026-01-01 00:15:00 2026-01-01 00:30:00  66.0 #> 3         L0 2026-01-01 00:30:00 2026-01-01 00:45:00  64.3 #> 4         L0 2026-01-01 00:45:00 2026-01-01 01:00:00  63.0 #> 5         L0 2026-01-01 01:00:00 2026-01-01 01:15:00  62.1 #> 6         L0 2026-01-01 01:15:00 2026-01-01 01:30:00  61.4  # supply custom holiday dates (e.g. only treat New Year's Day as a holiday) H0_custom <- slp_electricity("H0", start, end, holidays = "2026-01-01")  # Fetch state-level holidays from the nager.Date API and pass them in. # Each entry in the API response contains two relevant fields: #   $global  — logical; TRUE = nationwide holiday, FALSE = state-specific #   $counties — list of ISO 3166-2 state codes (e.g. "DE-BE" for Berlin) #               when global is FALSE; NULL otherwise # # Berlin (DE-BE) observes International Women's Day (March 8) in addition # to all nationwide holidays. The example below fetches 2027 holidays, # keeps entries where global is TRUE or "DE-BE" appears in counties, and # passes the resulting dates to slp_electricity(). if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ resp <- httr2::request("https://date.nager.at/api/v3") |>   httr2::req_url_path_append("PublicHolidays", "2027", "DE") |>   httr2::req_perform() |>   httr2::resp_body_json()  is_berlin <- \(x) isTRUE(x$global) || "DE-BE" %in% unlist(x$counties) holidays_berlin_2027 <- as.Date(   vapply(Filter(is_berlin, resp), \(x) x$date, character(1)) )  H0_berlin_2027 <- slp_electricity(   "H0", "2027-01-01", "2027-12-31",   holidays = holidays_berlin_2027 ) } # }  # consider only nationwide public holidays (default) H0_2026 <- slp_electricity("H0", start, end)  # electric power values are normalised to consumption of ~1,000 kWh/a sum(H0_2026$watts / 4 / 1000) #> [1] 998.1163  # convert watts to kWh per interval using a wrapper slp_generate_kwh <- \(...) {   out <- slp_electricity(...)   out$kwh <- out$watts / 4 / 1000   out } H0_kwh <- slp_generate_kwh("H0", start, end) head(H0_kwh) #>   profile_id          start_time            end_time     watts        kwh #> 1         H0 2026-01-01 00:00:00 2026-01-01 00:15:00 108.67764 0.02716941 #> 2         H0 2026-01-01 00:15:00 2026-01-01 00:30:00 100.72864 0.02518216 #> 3         H0 2026-01-01 00:30:00 2026-01-01 00:45:00  93.15226 0.02328806 #> 4         H0 2026-01-01 00:45:00 2026-01-01 01:00:00  85.82428 0.02145607 #> 5         H0 2026-01-01 01:00:00 2026-01-01 01:15:00  78.74471 0.01968618 #> 6         H0 2026-01-01 01:15:00 2026-01-01 01:30:00  72.28615 0.01807154

title: Generate a Standard Load Profile for Gas — slp_gas
text: https://www.bdew.de/energie/standardlastprofile-gas/ BDEW/VKU/GEODE. Leitfaden Abwicklung von Standardlastprofilen Gas, Kooperationsvereinbarung Gas, Annex XIV.2, 2025-10-28, Appendix 6. https://www.bdew.de/media/documents/251028_LF_SLP_Gas_KoV_XIV.2.pdf

title: Generate a Standard Load Profile for Gas — slp_gas
text: profile_id gas load profile identifier, required. One "HEF", "HMF", "HKO", "GKO", "GHA", "GMK", "GBD", "GBH", "GWA", "GGA", "GBA", "GGB", "GPD", "GMF", "GHD". Multiple values supported. dates Date vector character vector ISO 8601 format ("YYYY-MM-DD"). element start date gas day (German: Gastag, 06:00–06:00). Must length temperatures. temperatures numeric vector daily temperatures degrees Celsius, one value per gas day. Must length dates. Two options computing daily temperature supported Leitfaden (see Details): Simple daily mean (Tagesmitteltemperatur): arithmetic average hourly values gas day. Geometrically-weighted 4-day mean: recommended BDEW distribution network operators. production contexts, distribution network operators increasingly use gas forecast temperature (German: Gasprognosetemperatur, GPT) published DWD DTN instead raw daily mean. GPT incorporates multi-day weighted average seasonal adjustment reduces systematic seasonal bias pure temperature-based profiles (VKU SLP evaluation reports 2023, 2025). function accepts whichever temperature values passed; choice method caller's responsibility. kundenwert numeric scalar, required. Customer value (Kundenwert) kWh/day — daily gas consumption reference temperature 8 °C. Derive full reference year slp_gas_kundenwert(), supply value already know. See Details. variant SigLinDe variant (German: Ausprägung) use. Either "34" (default) "33". Variant 34 57 % linear component steeper heating slope; variant 33 45 % linear component. BDEW Leitfaden recommends distribution network operators test variants grid data select better fit. See Details. "HKO" profile pure sigmoid linear part unaffected argument. holidays controls public holiday treatment: NULL (default): built-nationwide German holidays used. NA: dates treated public holidays. character Date vector ISO 8601 format ("YYYY-MM-DD"): dates treated public holidays; built-data ignored entirely.

title: Generate a Standard Load Profile for Gas — slp_gas
text: data.frame three variables: profile_id, character, gas load profile identifier date, Date, start date gas day (06:00 local time) kwh, numeric, estimated gas consumption kWh gas day

title: Generate a Standard Load Profile for Gas — slp_gas
text: (German) gas market, standard load profiles (Standardlastprofile, SLP) used allocate gas volumes low-pressure customers continuously metered. synthetic procedure computes daily gas quantity : $$Q(D) = KW \times h(\vartheta_D) \times F_{WT}$$ : \(KW\) customer-specific scaling factor kWh/day (German: Kundenwert). \(h(\vartheta_D)\) SigLinDe profile function value daily temperature \(\vartheta_D\). \(F_{WT}\) weekday factor profile day type.

title: Generate a Standard Load Profile for Gas — slp_gas
text: SigLinDe function defined two variants (German: Ausprägungen). pure sigmoid term introduced TU München (Geiger / Hellwig 2002); linear envelope top — together 33 / 34 variant split — added FfE 2015 research report Weiterentwicklung des Standard- lastprofilverfahrens Gas (Appendix 7.1). current operational coefficient set published BDEW Leitfaden, Appendix 6 (2025-10-28): $$h(\vartheta) = \frac{}{1 + \left(\frac{B}{\vartheta - \vartheta_0}\right)^C} + D + \max(m_H \vartheta + b_H,\; m_W \vartheta + b_W)$$ first four terms form sigmoid part; last term linear part (space-heating hot water lines). Variant 34 (57 % linear component, steeper heating slope) default. Variant 33 (45 % linear component) alternative distribution network areas fits better. Distribution network operators advised test grid data. HKO profile (Kochgasprofil) pure sigmoid retained pre-SigLinDe era; 33/34 variant linear part always zero.

title: Generate a Standard Load Profile for Gas — slp_gas
text: daily temperature can computed two ways: Simple daily mean — arithmetic mean hourly temperatures: $$\vartheta_D = \frac{1}{24} \sum_{h=1}^{24} T_h$$ Geometrically-weighted 4-day mean (recommended BDEW network operators): $$\vartheta_D = \frac{T_D + 0.5 \times T_{D-1} + 0.25 \times T_{D-2} + 0.125 \times T_{D-3}}{1.875}$$ function accepts whichever temperature values user provides temperatures; choice method user's responsibility.

title: Generate a Standard Load Profile for Gas — slp_gas
text: Kundenwert \(KW\) scales dimensionless profile customer's actual consumption required input. recommended workflow two steps: Derive \(KW\) full reference year temperatures slp_gas_kundenwert(): $$KW = \frac{Q_a}{\sum_D h(\vartheta_D) \times F_{WT,D}}$$ \(Q_a\) annual consumption. Pass \(KW\) slp_gas() period want generate. Keeping two steps separate deliberate: kundenwert property customer climate zone, computed representative (ideally multi-year) temperature mean. Deriving short series generating collapse seasonal denominator bias result — single day \(h\) values cancel entirely.

title: Generate a Standard Load Profile for Gas — slp_gas
text: 15 gas profile IDs defined BDEW Leitfaden: Residential: HEF: single-family home (Einfamilienhaus) HMF: multi-family home (Mehrfamilienhaus) HKO: cooking hot water (Kochen / Warmwasser) Commercial / industrial: GKO: small commercial (Kleinstgewerbe) GHA: trade commerce (Handel) GMK: metal automotive (Metall / Kfz) GBD: services (Dienstleistung) GBH: accommodation (Beherbergung) GWA: laundries (Wäscherei) GGA: gastronomy (Gastronomie) GBA: bakeries (Bäckerei) GGB: mixed commercial (gemischtes Gewerbe) GPD: paper printing (Papier / Druck) GMF: large multi-family / mixed use (Mehrfamilienhaus groß) GHD: trade, commerce services aggregate (GHD-Stützpunkt)

title: Generate a Standard Load Profile for Gas — slp_gas
text: Unlike electricity profiles, gas weekday factors use seven individual weekdays (Mo, Tu, , Th, Fr, Sa, Su) rather three day types. Public holidays treated Sunday (Su); 24 December 31 December treated Saturday (Sa) unless fall Sunday. residential profiles HEF, HMF, HKO weekday factors 1, meaning day--week differentiation applied. built-holiday data cover years 1990 2099. dates outside range, holidays = NULL yield public holiday adjustments; pass holidays explicitly needed.

what: Compute the Kundenwert for a Gas Standard Load Profile — slp_gas_kundenwert
title: Compute the Kundenwert for a Gas Standard Load Profile — slp_gas_kundenwert
text: Compute kundenwert (customer value) scales gas standard load profile specific annual consumption. result can passed directly slp_gas() via kundenwert argument, enabling two-step workflow: derive kundenwert representative full-year reference temperature series, generate profiles shorter period using fixed value.

title: Compute the Kundenwert for a Gas Standard Load Profile — slp_gas_kundenwert
code: slp_gas_kundenwert(   profile_id,   dates = NULL,   temperatures = NULL,   annual_consumption = 1000,   variant = c("34", "33"),   holidays = NULL )

title: Compute the Kundenwert for a Gas Standard Load Profile — slp_gas_kundenwert
text: profile_id gas load profile identifier, required. values slp_gas(). Multiple values supported; result named numeric vector one element per profile. dates Date vector character vector ISO 8601 format ("YYYY-MM-DD"). meaningful kundenwert series ideally cover 365 (366) days. Must length temperatures. temperatures numeric vector daily temperatures degrees Celsius. Must length dates. annual_consumption numeric scalar, annual gas consumption kWh. Defaults 1000. variant SigLinDe variant, either "34" (default) "33". Must match variant passed slp_gas() applying resulting Kundenwert. holidays controls public holiday treatment. semantics slp_gas(). reference year used apply holiday calendar generation step.

title: Compute the Kundenwert for a Gas Standard Load Profile — slp_gas_kundenwert
text: named numeric vector length length(profile_id). element Kundenwert kWh/day corresponding profile. Names match input profile_id values.

title: Compute the Kundenwert for a Gas Standard Load Profile — slp_gas_kundenwert
text: Kundenwert derived annual consumption year's temperature profile: $$KW = \frac{Q_a}{\sum_D h(\vartheta_D) \cdot F_{WT,D}}$$ \(Q_a\) annual_consumption (annual consumption total; German: Jahresverbrauchsprognose, JVP) sum \(\sum_D h(\vartheta_D) \cdot F_{WT,D}\) runs days temperature weekday factor series. result meaningful denominator must reflect full seasonal cycle (ideally calendar year).

title: Compute the Kundenwert for a Gas Standard Load Profile — slp_gas_kundenwert
text: robust Kundenwert temperature series represent full reference year, ideally multi-year climatological mean rather single year, individual-year anomaly distorts scaling factor; fewer 365 days message shown. Daily mean temperatures can downloaded DWD (Deutscher Wetterdienst) open-data archive, e.g. via rdwd package. gas SLP article package website walks fetching DWD data, deriving Kundenwert, generating profiles.

title: Compute the Kundenwert for a Gas Standard Load Profile — slp_gas_kundenwert
text: slp_gas() requires kundenwert. already know , compute first slp_gas_kundenwert() full reference year customer's annual consumption, pass result slp_gas() generate profile whatever period need:
code: # Step 1 — derive KW from a full-year reference temperature series kw <- slp_gas_kundenwert("HEF", dates_year, temps_year, annual_consumption = 15000)  # Step 2 — generate a profile for any shorter period slp_gas("HEF", dates_jan_mar, temps_jan_mar, kundenwert = kw)

title: Compute the Kundenwert for a Gas Standard Load Profile — slp_gas_kundenwert
code: # Derive KW from a full-year reference temperature series dates_ref <- seq.Date(as.Date("2024-01-01"), as.Date("2024-12-31"), by = "day") doy       <- as.integer(format(dates_ref, "%j"))  # fake temperature data for demonstration here only temps_ref <- 10 - 11 * cos(2 * pi * (doy - 15) / 365) slp_gas_kundenwert("HEF", dates = dates_ref, temperatures = temps_ref,                    annual_consumption = 15000) #>      HEF  #> 43.13977   # Multiple profiles at once slp_gas_kundenwert(c("HEF", "GKO", "GWA"), dates_ref, temps_ref,                    annual_consumption = 15000) #>      HEF      GKO      GWA  #> 43.13977 41.23944 41.45100

title: Compute Dimensionless Daily Heating Demand (SigLinDe) — slp_gas_siglinde
text: theta Numeric vector daily mean outdoor temperatures °C (daily temperature). , B, C, D Numeric scalars — sigmoid coefficients. theta0 Numeric scalar — pole temperature (40 °C published profiles). function undefined \(\vartheta = \vartheta_0\) physically meaningless . mH, bH Numeric scalars — slope intercept heating linear component (Heizgas-Gerade). mW, bW Numeric scalars — slope intercept hot-water linear component (Warmwasser-Gerade).

text: numeric vector length theta giving dimensionless profile value \(h(\vartheta)\) temperature.

title: Compute Dimensionless Daily Heating Demand (SigLinDe) — slp_gas_siglinde
text: function value sum sigmoid part linear part: $$h(\vartheta) = \frac{}{1 + \left(\frac{B}{\vartheta -   \vartheta_0}\right)^C} + D + \max(m_H \vartheta + b_H,\;   m_W \vartheta + b_W)$$ sigmoid captures non-linear relationship outdoor temperature heating demand. linear envelope two lines represents space-heating demand (Heizgas-Gerade, slope mH) hot-water demand (Warmwasser-Gerade, slope mW). residential profiles (e.g. HEF, HMF) parts contribute. HKO (TUM) profile linear coefficients zero, sigmoid part remains. low-level building block used internally slp_gas(). exported users custom region-specific coefficients (e.g. state-level parameters BW_HEF03 Baden-Württemberg) can compute \(h(\vartheta)\) directly build profiles. Published coefficients 15 standard profiles listed SigLinDe parameters article.

title: Compute Dimensionless Daily Heating Demand (SigLinDe) — slp_gas_siglinde

Abwicklung von Standardlastprofilen Gas, Kooperationsvereinbarung Gas, Annex XIV.2, 2025-10-28. unified SigLinDe profile function shown p. 42 (Abbildung 12; PDF page 54); per-profile coefficients tabulated Appendix 6, pp. 145–166. https://www.bdew.de/media/documents/251028_LF_SLP_Gas_KoV_XIV.2.pdf

what: standardlastprofile: BDEW Standard Load Profiles for Electricity and Gas — standardlastprofile-package
title: standardlastprofile: BDEW Standard Load Profiles for Electricity and Gas — standardlastprofile-package
text: Provides representative standard load profiles (SLPs) electricity gas published German Association Energy Water Industries (BDEW Bundesverband der Energie- und Wasserwirtschaft e.V.) tidy format. electricity profiles cover 1999 profiles — households (H0), commercial (G0–G6), agriculture (L0–L2) — updated 2025 profiles (H25, G25, L25, P25, S25), additionally represent households photovoltaic systems battery storage. interface generates electricity load profile user-defined date range. second interface generates daily gas load profiles using BDEW/VKU/GEODE synthetic 'SigLinDe' procedure daily temperatures customer value ('Kundenwert'). 1999 data methodology described VDEW (1999), "Repräsentative VDEW-Lastprofile", https://www.bdew.de/media/documents/1999_Repraesentative-VDEW-Lastprofile.pdf. electricity generation algorithm described VDEW (2000), "Anwendung der Repräsentativen VDEW-Lastprofile step--step", https://www.bdew.de/media/documents/2000131_Anwendung-repraesentativen_Lastprofile-Step--step.pdf. 2025 profiles described BDEW (2025), "Standardlastprofile Strom", https://www.bdew.de/energie/standardlastprofile-strom/. gas procedure described BDEW (2025), "Standardlastprofile Gas", https://www.bdew.de/energie/standardlastprofile-gas/.

title: standardlastprofile 2.1.0
text: slp_generate() now defunct. Calling raises error. Use slp_electricity() instead (superseded since 2.0.0). slp dataset alias now defunct. Accessing slp raises error. Use slp_electricity_profiles instead (deprecated since 2.0.0).

title: standardlastprofile 2.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-06-11 release (finally) adds gas standard load profiles (SLPs) alongside existing electricity profiles. introduce updated interface electricity SLPs . renames deprecations backward compatible.

title: standardlastprofile 2.0.0
text: slp_electricity() new primary function generating electricity SLPs. replaces slp_generate() cleaner interface: state_code argument restriction date range (previously limited 1990–2073 built-holiday data). slp_gas() implements BDEW/VKU/GEODE synthetic procedure gas SLPs (SigLinDe method). supports 15 gas profile IDs defined BDEW Leitfaden, 2025-10-28 (HEF, HMF, HKO, GKO, GHA, GMK, GBD, GBH, GWA, GGA, GBA, GGB, GPD, GMF, GHD). function takes daily temperatures kundenwert (kWh/day), returns daily gas consumption kWh. variant argument selects SigLinDe variant (German: “Ausprägung”) "34" (57 % linear component) "33" (45 % linear component). slp_gas_kundenwert() derives kundenwert full reference year daily temperatures annual consumption target. might use two-step workflow: compute KW representative year, pass result kundenwert slp_gas() shorter period. Daily mean temperatures can obtained e.g. DWD open-data archive, e.g. via rdwd package; see gas SLP article package website complete fetch--profile walkthrough. slp_gas_siglinde() exposes low-level dimensionless daily heating demand function h(θ) used internally slp_gas(). exported users custom region-specific coefficients (e.g. state-level parameters BW_HEF03 Baden-Württemberg) can compute h(θ) directly build profiles. slp_gas_coefficients() returns SigLinDe profile function coefficients (, B, C, D, θ₀, mH, bH, mW, bW) one gas profiles data frame. Supports variants ("34", "33"). slp_gas_weekday_factors() returns weekday scaling factors (F_WT) one gas profiles data frame columns profile_id, day, f_wt. Nationwide German public holidays now computed via Anonymous Gregorian Easter algorithm rather looked precomputed table. Coverage open-ended 1990 onward (year German Unity Day introduced); previous 2073 / 2099 caps longer apply. State-level holidays longer included; use holidays argument supply custom dates. slp_info() now accepts gas profile IDs (HEF, HMF, HKO, etc.) addition electricity IDs, respects language argument . Electricity gas IDs can mixed freely single call.
